steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests cross-text analysis. Text 1's researchers suggest magpies without trackers pecked at another's tracker to help it — a claim of altruistic intent. Text 2 cautions that animals may peck at equipment simply out of curiosity about its novelty, not to help. The correct answer must reflect how Text 2's author would push back on Text 1's interpretation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'That behavior might have been due to the novelty of the magpies' captive setting rather than to the novelty of the tracker.': Text 2 attributes curiosity to the novelty of the equipment itself, not to the captive setting; this misrepresents Text 2's argument. ✗
  • Choice B — 'That behavior likely indicates that the magpies were deliberately attempting to benefit themselves by obtaining the tracker.': Text 2 never suggests self-interested motives; it proposes curiosity, not self-benefit, as the alternative explanation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'That behavior may not be evidence of selflessness in Gymnorhina tibicen because not all the captured magpies demonstrated it.': Text 2 does not argue about frequency or how many birds participated; it disputes the interpretation of intent, not the count. ✗
  • Choice D — 'That behavior might be adequately explained without suggesting that the magpies were attempting to assist the other magpie.': directly reflects Text 2's central point that curiosity about novel equipment is a sufficient alternative explanation, requiring no assumption of deliberate assistance. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
